description: Iteratively parse all 153 test recipes in batches of 20, evaluating and fixing the AI prompt after each batch. Each batch benefits from all prior prompt improvements. Final step: re-parse all 153 with the polished prompt and sync to recipes.ts.

userStories.title: Create batch parsing script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable script for batch parsing recipes through the edge function, so that I can parse slices of recipes efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs that reads TEST_RECIPES from recipes.ts
Accepts --offset N and --limit N flags to parse a slice of recipes (e.g. --offset 0 --limit 20 for first 20)
Accepts --force flag to re-parse recipes that already have completed status

For each recipe: upsert into 'recipes' table via REST API (POST http://127.0.0.1:54321/rest/v1/recipes with Prefer: resolution=merge-duplicates). Fields: {name, url, created_by: null, event_id: null, ingredient_id: null}. Use ON CONFLICT on name to get existing ID
For each recipe: call parse-recipe edge function (POST http://127.0.0.1:54321/functions/v1/parse-recipe) with {recipeId, recipeUrl, recipeName}
Process with 10 concurrent workers using Promise.all on batched chunks
Auth headers: apikey and Authorization both 'REDACTED'
Log progress per recipe and print summary (parsed, failed, skipped, elapsed time)
Script runs successfully with --limit 0 (no-op): node test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs --limit 0
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: ALREADY COMPLETED. Do not re-implement. The script exists at test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs and works correctly.

userStories.title: Replace evaluation script with Claude-powered evaluator
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the evaluation script to use Claude's linguistic knowledge instead of brittle regex rules, so that ingredient quality issues are detected more accurately with correct suggested fixes.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs with a Claude-powered version
Script queries recipe_ingredients and recipes from local Supabase REST API (same as before)
Groups ingredients by recipe and sends them to Claude (Anthropic API) in batches for evaluation

Claude evaluates each ingredient for: (1) pluralization issues (e.g. 'tomatoes' should be 'tomato'), (2) prep adjectives that should be removed vs kept (e.g. remove 'toasted' from 'toasted sesame seed' but keep 'dried' in 'dried apricot'), (3) category correctness (water should be 'other' not 'beverages', canned tomato products should be 'pantry', fresh produce should be 'produce'), (4) unit standardization (use tsp/tbsp/cup/oz/lb/quart/gallon/head/bunch/stalk/clove/sprig/piece/slice/strip/can/ear/null), (5) typos and misspellings, (6) quantity reasonableness, (7) count units embedded in names (e.g. 'garlic clove' should be name='garlic' unit='clove')
Uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from environment or from supabase/functions/.env (same pattern as test-combine/parse-recipes.mjs lines 19-21)

Outputs same JSON format to test-combine/evaluation-report.json: {summary: {totalIssues, totalRecipes, totalIngredients, byType}, issues: [{recipeId, recipeName, ingredientName, issueType, description, suggestedFix}]}
Processes recipes in batches of 10-20 per Claude API call to stay within token limits
Script handles empty DB gracefully (0 recipes = 0 issues)
Script runs successfully against the 24 already-parsed recipes: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: COMPLETED. Replaced regex-based evaluator with Claude-powered version using claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929. Batches of 15 recipes per API call, 1.5s delay. Found 58 issues across 43 recipes (563 ingredients) vs old evaluator's 24 issues. Claude correctly handles singularization, product-form qualifiers, context-dependent categories. Output format unchanged.

userStories.title: Parse batch 1 (recipes 1-20), evaluate, and fix prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to parse the first 20 recipes, evaluate the output, and fix the AI prompt for the most common issues so that subsequent batches benefit from improvements.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs --offset 0 --limit 20
Verify 20 recipes parsed successfully (check parse log output)
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs
Read evaluation-report.json and identify ALL issue types and their frequency
Fix the AI prompts in BOTH files based on findings: test-combine/parse-recipes.mjs (SYSTEM_PROMPT) and supabase/functions/parse-recipe/index.ts (systemPrompt)
The ingredient parsing rules in both prompts must be identical (structural differences for description/servings/instructions are OK)
Document in activity.md: issue counts by type, specific prompt changes made, before counts
Existing edge function tests pass: npx vitest run tests/unit/edge-functions/parse-recipe.test.ts
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: ALREADY COMPLETED. Batch 1 was parsed and evaluated. Prompt fixes were applied to both files: singularization rules, category rules (water→other, seeds→spices, lemon juice→produce, canned tomato→pantry), product-form qualifier preservation (dried apricot, dry wine, crushed tomato, red pepper flakes), prep adjective removal (toasted sesame seed), non-standard unit conversion (dash/pinch→tsp or null), metric conversion reinforcement. 35 issues found initially, reduced to 24 after evaluator improvements. See activity.md for full details.

userStories.title: Re-evaluate batch 1 with Claude-powered evaluator and fix prompt if needed
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to re-evaluate the already-parsed batch 1 recipes using the new Claude-powered evaluator to get a more accurate baseline, and fix any newly-discovered issues in the prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs (now Claude-powered)
Compare findings against the previous regex-based evaluation (35 issues initially, 24 after evaluator tweaks)
Identify any new issue types that the regex evaluator missed
Identify any false positives from the regex evaluator that Claude correctly ignores
IF Claude finds new issue types: fix the prompt (both files) to address them
Document in activity.md: Claude evaluator results vs regex evaluator results, any new prompt changes
Edge function tests pass if prompt was changed: npx vitest run tests/unit/edge-functions/parse-recipe.test.ts
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: COMPLETED. Analyzed Claude evaluator report (58 raw issues) vs regex evaluator (24 issues). After filtering, 44 true issues and 14 false positives. Claude found 3 new issue types: quantity_precision (4), count_unit_in_name (3), typo (1). Prompt fixes applied to both files: quantity rounding to 2 decimals, dried fruits->pantry, taco shells->bakery, pickled items as product-form qualifiers. Evaluator improved: ginger=produce, tofu=meat_seafood, better quantity precision rules.

userStories.title: Parse batch 2 (recipes 21-40), evaluate, and fix if needed
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to parse recipes 21-40 with the improved prompt and check if the fixes from batch 1 resolved the issues, fixing any new issue types found.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs --offset 20 --limit 20
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs
Compare issue counts against batch 1: the issue types fixed in US-003 should appear less or not at all in the new batch
IF new issue types are found that weren't in batch 1: fix the prompt (both files) to address them
IF no new issues: document 'no new issue types found, prompt is holding' in activity.md
Document in activity.md: issue counts, whether prompt was changed, comparison to batch 1
Edge function tests pass if prompt was changed: npx vitest run tests/unit/edge-functions/parse-recipe.test.ts
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: COMPLETED. All 20 batch 2 recipes were already parsed (from prior session with US-003 prompt). Evaluation: 18 raw issues, 7 true issues, 11 false positives. Key finding: prompt fixes from US-003/US-004 are effective — category_inconsistency dropped 92% (13→1), pluralization dropped 100% (7→0), prep_adjective dropped 100% (2→0). All 7 remaining true issues already addressed by current US-004 prompt (recipe unit, inch unit, 0.333 precision, taco shell category). No prompt changes needed. Will be fixed on full re-parse.

userStories.title: Parse batch 3 (recipes 41-60), evaluate, and fix if needed
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to parse recipes 41-60 and continue the iterative improvement cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs --offset 40 --limit 20
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs
IF new issue types found: fix prompt (both files)
IF no new issues in the batch 3 recipes: document 'prompt is stable' in activity.md
Document comparison to previous batches in activity.md
Edge function tests pass if prompt was changed
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: COMPLETED. All 20 batch 3 recipes parsed successfully. Evaluation: 29 raw issues, 3 true issues, 26 false positives (90% FP rate). No new issue types found. Prompt is stable for 2 consecutive batches. True issues: crispy onion (prep_adjective), green cardamom pod (count_unit_in_name), pork sausage crumbles (pluralization) — all already covered by existing prompt rules. Category_inconsistency dropped to 0 in batch 3.

userStories.title: Parse batch 4 (recipes 61-80), evaluate, and fix if needed
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to parse recipes 61-80 and continue verifying prompt quality.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs --offset 60 --limit 20
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs
IF new issue types found: fix prompt (both files)
IF no new issues in batch 4 recipes: document 'prompt stable for 2+ batches'
Document in activity.md
Edge function tests pass if prompt was changed
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: COMPLETED. All 20 batch 4 recipes parsed successfully (3 runs needed for BOOT_ERROR/rate limits). Evaluation: 20 raw issues, 1 true issue (0.125 quantity precision), 19 false positives (95% FP rate). No new issue types found. Prompt stable for 3 consecutive batches. The single true issue (0.125 should be 0.13) is already covered by existing prompt rules — an AI execution edge case, not a systemic failure. No prompt changes needed.

acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs --offset 80 --limit 20
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs
IF new issue types found: fix prompt (both files)
IF no new issues: document stability in activity.md
Edge function tests pass if prompt was changed
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: COMPLETED. All 20 batch 5 recipes parsed successfully (3 runs for BOOT_ERROR/rate limits). Evaluation: 19 raw issues, 2 true issues (frozen chopped spinach prep_adjective, tomato paste category), 17 false positives (89% FP rate). No new issue types found. Prompt stable for 4 consecutive batches. Both true issues already covered by existing prompt rules — AI execution edge cases, not systemic failures. No prompt changes needed.

userStories.title: Parse batch 6 (recipes 101-120), evaluate, and fix if needed

Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs

IF no new issues: document stability in activity.md
Edge function tests pass if prompt was changed

userStories.notes: COMPLETED. All 20 batch 6 recipes parsed successfully (3 runs for BOOT_ERROR/rate limits). 'Double Tofu Caesar Sandwich' returned 0 ingredients (Instagram Reel URL — not parseable). Evaluation: 13 raw issues, 1 true issue (saltine cracker unit='sleeve' non-standard), 12 false positives (92% FP rate). No new issue types found. Prompt stable for 5 consecutive batches. No prompt changes needed.

userStories.title: Parse final batch (recipes 121-153), evaluate, and fix if needed
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to parse the remaining 33 recipes and complete the initial pass through all 153.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs --offset 120 --limit 33
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs
Verify all 153 recipes have been parsed (query recipe_content count)
IF new issue types found: fix prompt (both files)
Document final initial-pass statistics in activity.md: total issues remaining by type across all 153 recipes
Edge function tests pass if prompt was changed
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: COMPLETED. All 153 recipes attempted. 148 parsed with ingredients, 5 have permanently broken/blocked URLs (404, 403, 429, image error), 1 Instagram Reel (0 ingredients). DB: 154 recipes, 1898 ingredients. Evaluation: 111 raw issues (3 evaluator batches failed), ~30-40 true issues. Most true issues from batches 1-2 (pre-fix prompts). No new issue types. Prompt stable for 6 consecutive batches. No prompt changes needed.

userStories.title: Full re-parse of all 153 recipes with final prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to re-parse ALL 153 recipes using the finalized prompt so that every recipe benefits from all accumulated prompt improvements.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: node test-combine/scripts/batch-parse.mjs --force (re-parse all 153)
Run: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs
Total issues should be near zero — if not, make final prompt adjustments and re-parse once more
Save final report as test-combine/final-evaluation-report.json
Document in activity.md: final issue counts, comparison to initial batch 1 counts, overall improvement percentage
Edge function tests pass: npx vitest run tests/unit/edge-functions/parse-recipe.test.ts
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: This is the validation step. With 10 concurrent workers, re-parsing all 153 takes ~5-8 minutes. Every recipe is now parsed with the final, polished prompt. The evaluation should show dramatic improvement over batch 1. If issues remain, they're likely edge cases — make one final round of prompt tweaks and re-parse once more if needed. The --force flag must clear existing recipe_ingredients and recipe_content before re-parsing each recipe.

userStories.title: Sync improved parsed data to recipes.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recipes.ts updated with the final high-quality parsed ingredient data so that the test data reflects the best AI output.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test-combine/scripts/sync-to-recipes.mjs that reads parsed ingredients from DB and updates recipes.ts
For each recipe in DB: query recipe_ingredients ordered by sort_order, format as TestRecipe.ingredients format ({name, quantity, unit, category, rawText})
Map DB field raw_text → TypeScript rawText
Preserve recipe order, id, name, cuisine, url in recipes.ts — only update ingredients arrays
Write recipes.ts maintaining existing formatting (type/interface exports, const structure)
Run final evaluation: node test-combine/scripts/evaluate-parsed.mjs to confirm consistency
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: Reuse patterns from existing parse-recipes.mjs: formatIngredients() (line 206) and updateRecipeInSource() (line 214) show how to write ingredients back to the file. Match each DB recipe to the recipes.ts entry by name (the batch-parse script inserts with the same name). The sync must handle all 153 recipes. Read recipes.ts, for each recipe block find it by id, replace the ingredients array, write back.
